creatures.name: Sark
creatures.description: Former humans who completed the Forgetting, appearing human but with wrong gait and posture, often carrying tools they no longer understand.
creatures.behavior: Territorial pack hunters that claim abandoned places, using cunning ambush tactics and flanking maneuvers.
creatures.habitat: Ruins, overgrown farmsteads, forest hollows, and anywhere civilization has retreated from.

creatures.name: Trow
creatures.description: Sarks left alone in darkness so long they've become shadow-things, seen only at torchlight's edge or in the corner of vision.
creatures.behavior: Patient stalkers that accelerate the Forgetting in others, isolating victims over months or years before claiming them.
creatures.habitat: Dark places where Sarks have dwelt undisturbed for generations.

creatures.name: Kestane
creatures.description: Hound-sized jade gargoyles with angular forms, green eyes, and claws that drip acidic jade.
creatures.behavior: Once threshold guardians, now they skitter in packs without purpose, attacking intruders with claws and stone teeth.
creatures.habitat: Ancient shamanic sites, old temples, and ruins where wards were once carved.

creatures.name: Bhruggspire
creatures.description: Man-height floating jade sentinels with grinding stone wings, patrolling ancient corridors in endless circuits.
creatures.behavior: Patrol fixed routes ceaselessly, hurling bullets of dripping jade acid at any who intrude.
creatures.habitat: Interior corridors of old shamanic fortresses and sealed ritual chambers.

creatures.name: Grundkholm
creatures.description: Massive jade constructs twice a man's height, carved from red-veined stone with knuckles that seep continuous acid.
creatures.behavior: Fought like a collapsing wall—slow but devastating, requiring distance and positioning rather than direct combat.
creatures.habitat: The deepest vaults and most sacred chambers of ancient shamanic strongholds.

creatures.name: Skrelving
creatures.description: Flightless birds tall as a man's chest with rust-red feathers and iron-chisel beaks, hunting by sound and heat.
creatures.behavior: Hunt in flocks through underground spaces; a single bird is a nuisance, a flock is lethal.
creatures.habitat: Deep underground places, caverns, and subterranean ruins.

creatures.name: Ruddyman
creatures.description: A Skrelving grown horse-tall by cannibalizing its flock, with blackened feathers and a war-pick beak.
creatures.behavior: Emerges when prey grows scarce, remembering everything it has consumed and hunting with terrible focus.
creatures.habitat: Deep places where Skrelving flocks have exhausted their prey.

creatures.name: Khernghast
creatures.description: Child-sized creatures with peat-smoke leather skin and milky quartz eyes, dwelling in ancient barrows.
creatures.behavior: Territorial but transactional—offerings of milk, grain, or iron buy safe passage; theft or disrespect brings lingering misfortune.
creatures.habitat: Barrows, old cairns, and burial mounds where the dead were laid with ceremony.

creatures.name: Grethling
creatures.description: Gaunt grey-green figures with fish-scale skin that emerge from coastal fog, reeking of rotting kelp.
creatures.behavior: Raiders who come specifically to abduct spouses; silver coins cause them to pause in covetous fascination.
creatures.habitat: Tidal caves, fog-shrouded beaches, and coastal shallows—never beyond the sea's reach.

creatures.name: Hound of Klokhanwast
creatures.description: A massive black hound with light-drinking eyes that appears only in the darkness of ruined strongholds.
creatures.behavior: Observes silently; those who flee are spared, those who linger are found dead of terror with no visible wounds.
creatures.habitat: Ruined fortifications and forsaken holds where lords once ruled.

creatures.name: Mossen Ghrelda
creatures.description: A twice-man-height ogress with milk-curdled skin, filed teeth, and pelts stitched from unidentifiable hides.
creatures.behavior: Waits motionless along forest paths for days until prey wanders close, preferring victims alive for what follows.
creatures.habitat: Dense forest where canopy blocks the sky, overgrown ruins, anywhere shadow pools permanently.

creatures.name: Dhoor Puddle
creatures.description: A formless mass like waterlogged sand or blanched jelly with a single vast cataracted eye, leaving brine and corruption in its wake.
creatures.behavior: Arrives with darkness, curdling sleep into fever-visions; blades pass through it uselessly but fire repels and names wound it.
creatures.habitat: Coastlines, salt-marshes, and wherever land and water meet uncertainly.

creatures.name: Vlimmen
creatures.description: Small wrongly-proportioned figures with phosphorescent skin, smelling of crushed grass and lightning, whose laughter sounds like water over stones.
creatures.behavior: Tricksters who lead travelers astray, pinch out torches, and steal first objects then memories; milk and honey may buy forbearance.
creatures.habitat: Liminal spaces—crossroads, forest edges, thresholds between one thing and another.

creatures.name: Malkh
creatures.description: A mastiff-sized black cat with a pale chest blazon and phosphorescent eyes, whose purr resonates through bone.
creatures.behavior: Appears near farmsteads; those who leave milk find livestock protected, those who neglect or harm it suffer wasting sickness and curdled stores.
creatures.habitat: Farmsteads, barns, and wherever cattle are kept.

creatures.name: Thrymaxia
creatures.description: A giantess twice a tall man's height with bruise-colored skin and rime-like hair, carrying a light-drinking black staff.
creatures.behavior: Does not hunt—simply exists, and killing winter follows wherever she walks, reshaping the land itself.
creatures.habitat: Mountain passes, frozen lakes, high places where nothing grows and warmth is forgotten.

creatures.name: Khroth
creatures.description: A grey ash-colored figure shaped like an old man, appearing at liminal hours and leaving no trace of presence.
creatures.behavior: A death omen that cannot be fought or bargained with—to see it means death approaches someone in your bloodline.
creatures.habitat: Anywhere the veil is thin, especially near ancestral dwellings.

creatures.name: Grellwife
creatures.description: A woman-like creature with grey-green lichen skin and hooved legs hidden beneath tattered green skirts, smelling of wet grass or stagnant water.
creatures.behavior: Dual-natured—guards some farmsteads in exchange for offerings while haunting pools to drink blood from the unwary.
creatures.habitat: Farmsteads where she wards, forest pools and slow streams where she hunts.

creatures.name: Lhinnwife
creatures.description: A young woman in sodden ragged vestments standing motionless at water's edge, dripping ceaselessly though on dry ground.
creatures.behavior: Waits silently; those who stop to speak or reach toward her are found dead without wounds, drained of warmth.
creatures.habitat: Lakeshores, lonely paths beside water, places where travelers walk alone at night.

creatures.name: Burnt Vennghast
creatures.description: A shapeshifter appearing as stooped man, indeterminate beast, or char-colored greyhound, leaving the smell of scorched hair.
creatures.behavior: Terrorizes rather than kills, stalking lone travelers across days or weeks, changing form between each sighting until victims flee or break.
creatures.habitat: Hill passes, stone circles, lonely roads between settlements.

creatures.name: Hollgh
creatures.description: An angry or insulted ancestor spirit, invisible apart from liminal circumstances: shamans in trance, at the edge of sleep, in deep fog.
creatures.behavior: Attacks the senses - ringing ears, heavy limbs, dimming sight - and in numbers can cause totalsensory loss, leaving victims helpless to environmental dangers.
creatures.habitat: Barrow-mounds, old vaults, and any ground where the dead were buried carelessly or had their rest disturbed.

creatures.name: Ongkhan-hollgh
creatures.description: A bound spirit-helper gone feral and parasitic, contained within a vessel, polluting its surroundings.
creatures.behavior: Passively drains vitality from those nearby, causing rust, foul dreams, and sickness, while compelling people to touch or open its vessel, which leads to madness.
creatures.habitat: Abandoned shaman's huts, reliquaries in ruins, or unknowingly carried by traders as curious carvings or stoppered jars.
weakness: unknown: destroying the vessel does not kill the spirit

creatures.name: Ezn
creatures.description: An ancient and powerful master spirit of a place; it IS the place, its name cannot be known.
creatures.behavior: Does not act directly but when offended turns its domain hostile—streams go bitter, paths loop endlessly, weather turns cruel.
creatures.habitat: Bound to a singular geographic feature such as a mountain, megalith, deep bog, or cave; they never wander.
weakness: specific offerings or ritual silences, though the terms vary by Ezn and guessing wrong is itself a trespass
